revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 鴨山口神社 |画像 = [[File:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
境内 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[御所市]]櫛羅2428 |ISO = JP-23 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 27|緯度秒 = 40 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 54 |祭神 = [[大山祇命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（大）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 一間社[[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[7月16日]]（夏季大祭）
[[10月16日]]（秋季大祭） |神事 = |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[画像:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''鴨山口神社'''（かもやまぐちじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[御所市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内大社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == '''[[大山祇命]]'''を主祭神とし、[[天照大神|大日霊大神]]、御霊大神を配祀する。 == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建年代は不詳。[[延喜式]]では、[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]において「[[大和国]][[葛上郡]] 鴨山口神社 大 月次新嘗」とあるほか、祈雨祭神八十五座の一座に挙げられており、山口社は十四社あるがその内葛上郡の鴨山口神社が本社であるとされている。また、下鴨社（[[鴨都波神社]]）に対して上鴨社とも呼ばれていた。 [[近代社格制度]]では村社に列格した。 === 神階 === * [[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]、従五位下より正五位下（『[[日本三代実録]]』）- 表記は「鴨山口神」。 == 境内 == ファイル:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|250px|拝殿 ファイル:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Honden.jpg|250px|本殿 ファイル:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Sakuragisha.jpg|250px|桜木社（稲荷神社） ファイル:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Kasugajinja.jpg|250px|春日神社 ファイル:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Tenmanjinja.jpg|250px|天満神社 == 参考文献 == * 社頭案内板 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001412.html 鴨山口神社] - 御所市観光ガイド * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22710 鴨山口神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:かもやまくちにますしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かもやまくちにますしんしや]]

{{神社 |名称 = 鴨山口神社 |画像 = [[File:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
境内 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[御所市]]櫛羅2428 |ISO = JP-23 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 27|緯度秒 = 40 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 54 |祭神 = [[大山祇命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（大）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 一間社[[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[7月16日]]（夏季大祭）
[[10月16日]]（秋季大祭） |神事 = |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[画像:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''鴨山口神社'''（かもやまぐちじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[御所市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内大社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == '''[[大山祇命]]'''を主祭神とし、[[天照大神|大日霊大神]]、御霊大神を配祀する。 == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建年代は不詳。[[延喜式]]では、[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]において「[[大和国]][[葛上郡]] 鴨山口神社 大 月次新嘗」とあるほか、祈雨祭神八十五座の一座に挙げられており、山口社は十四社あるがその内葛上郡の鴨山口神社が本社であるとされている。また、下鴨社（[[鴨都波神社]]）に対して上鴨社とも呼ばれていた。 [[近代社格制度]]では村社に列格した。 === 神階 === * [[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]、従五位下より正五位下（『[[日本三代実録]]』）- 表記は「鴨山口神」。 == 境内 == ファイル:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|250px|拝殿 ファイル:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Honden.jpg|250px|本殿 ファイル:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Sakuragisha.jpg|250px|桜木社（稲荷神社） ファイル:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Kasugajinja.jpg|250px|春日神社 ファイル:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Tenmanjinja.jpg|250px|天満神社 == 参考文献 == * 社頭案内板 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001412.html 鴨山口神社] - 御所市観光ガイド * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22710 鴨山口神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:かもやまくちにますしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かもやまくちにますしんしや]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[御所市]]櫛羅2428 |ISO = JP-23 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 27|緯度秒 = 40 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 54 |祭神 = [[大山祇命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（大）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 一間社[[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[7月16日]]（夏季大祭）
[[10月16日]]（秋季大祭） |神事 = |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[画像:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''鴨山口神社'''（かもやまぐちじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[御所市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内大社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == '''[[大山祇命]]'''を主祭神とし、[[天照大神|大日霊大神]]、御霊大神を配祀する。 == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建年代は不詳。[[延喜式]]では、[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]において「[[大和国]][[葛上郡]] 鴨山口神社 大 月次新嘗」とあるほか、祈雨祭神八十五座の一座に挙げられており、山口社は十四社あるがその内葛上郡の鴨山口神社が本社であるとされている。また、下鴨社（[[鴨都波神社]]）に対して上鴨社とも呼ばれていた。 [[近代社格制度]]では村社に列格した。 === 神階 === * [[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]、従五位下より正五位下（『[[日本三代実録]]』）- 表記は「鴨山口神」。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === *木造大日霊命坐像（おおひるめおのみことざぞう）・御霊大神坐像（ごりょうおおかみざぞう）{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/0000001345.html |title=国指定文化財 |publisher=御所市 |accessdate=2019-08-25 }} == 境内 == Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Sakuragisha.jpg|桜木社（稲荷神社） Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Kasugajinja.jpg|春日神社 Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Tenmanjinja.jpg|天満神社 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 社頭案内板 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001412.html 鴨山口神社] - 御所市観光ガイド * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22710 鴨山口神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:かもやまくちにますしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かもやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]]

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine'''|鴨山口神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine'''|鴨山口神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{Infobox religious building

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine'''|鴨山口神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine | native_name = 鴨山口神社 | image = Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Keidai.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.461322|135.715404}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine'''|鴨山口神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine'''|鴨山口神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine'''|鴨山口神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine'''|鴨山口神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine'''|鴨山口神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine'''|鴨山口神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine'''|鴨山口神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

<!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Expand Japanese|鴨山口神社}}
{{Infobox religious building | name = Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine | native_name = 鴨山口神社 | image = Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Keidai.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.461322|135.715404}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine'''|鴨山口神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

<!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Expand Japanese|鴨山口神社}}
{{Infobox religious building | name = Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine | native_name = 鴨山口神社 | image = Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Keidai.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.461322|135.715404}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine'''|鴨山口神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].
== instance of (P31) ==

revision.comment: 6 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 鴨山口神社 |画像 = [[File:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
境内
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[御所市]]櫛羅2428 |ISO = JP-23 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 27|緯度秒 = 40 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 54 |祭神 = [[大山祇命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（大）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 一間社[[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[7月16日]]（夏季大祭）
[[10月16日]]（秋季大祭） |神事 = |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[画像:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''鴨山口神社'''（かもやまぐちじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[御所市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内大社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == '''[[大山祇命]]'''を主祭神とし、[[天照大神|大日霊大神]]、御霊大神を配祀する。 == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建年代は不詳。[[延喜式]]では、[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]において「[[大和国]][[葛上郡]] 鴨山口神社 大 月次新嘗」とあるほか、祈雨祭神八十五座の一座に挙げられており、山口社は十四社あるがその内葛上郡の鴨山口神社が本社であるとされている。また、下鴨社（[[鴨都波神社]]）に対して上鴨社とも呼ばれていた。 [[近代社格制度]]では村社に列格した。 === 神階 === * [[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]、従五位下より正五位下（『[[日本三代実録]]』）- 表記は「鴨山口神」。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === *木造大日霊命坐像（おおひるめおのみことざぞう）・御霊大神坐像（ごりょうおおかみざぞう）{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/0000001345.html |title=国指定文化財 |publisher=御所市 |accessdate=2019-08-25 }} == 境内 == Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Sakuragisha.jpg|桜木社（稲荷神社） Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Kasugajinja.jpg|春日神社 Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Tenmanjinja.jpg|天満神社 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 社頭案内板 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001412.html 鴨山口神社] - 御所市観光ガイド * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22710 鴨山口神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:かもやまくちにますしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かもやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]]

[[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|鴨山口神社|version=106904388|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 鴨山口神社 |画像 = [[File:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
境内
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[御所市]]櫛羅2428 |ISO = JP-23 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 27|緯度秒 = 40 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 54 |祭神 = [[大山祇命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（大）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 一間社[[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[7月16日]]（夏季大祭）
[[10月16日]]（秋季大祭） |神事 = |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[画像:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''鴨山口神社'''（かもやまぐちじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[御所市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内大社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == '''[[大山祇命]]'''を主祭神とし、[[天照大神|大日霊大神]]、御霊大神を配祀する。 == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建年代は不詳。[[延喜式]]では、[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]において「[[大和国]][[葛上郡]] 鴨山口神社 大 月次新嘗」とあるほか、祈雨祭神八十五座の一座に挙げられており、山口社は十四社あるがその内葛上郡の鴨山口神社が本社であるとされている。また、下鴨社（[[鴨都波神社]]）に対して上鴨社とも呼ばれていた。 [[近代社格制度]]では村社に列格した。 === 神階 === * [[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]、従五位下より正五位下（『[[日本三代実録]]』）- 表記は「鴨山口神」。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === *木造大日霊命坐像（おおひるめおのみことざぞう）・御霊大神坐像（ごりょうおおかみざぞう）{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/0000001345.html |title=国指定文化財 |publisher=御所市 |accessdate=2019-08-25 }} == 境内 == Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Sakuragisha.jpg|桜木社（稲荷神社） Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Kasugajinja.jpg|春日神社 Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Tenmanjinja.jpg|天満神社 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 社頭案内板 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001412.html 鴨山口神社] - 御所市観光ガイド * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22710 鴨山口神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:かもやまくちにますしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かもやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] ==End Japanese==

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[御所市]]櫛羅2428 |ISO = JP-23 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 27|緯度秒 = 40 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 54 |祭神 = [[大山祇命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（大）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 一間社[[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[7月16日]]（夏季大祭）
[[10月16日]]（秋季大祭） |神事 = |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[画像:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''鴨山口神社'''（かもやまぐちじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[御所市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内大社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == '''[[大山祇命]]'''を主祭神とし、[[天照大神|大日霊大神]]、御霊大神を配祀する。 == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建年代は不詳。[[延喜式]]では、[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]において「[[大和国]][[葛上郡]] 鴨山口神社 大 月次新嘗」とあるほか、祈雨祭神八十五座の一座に挙げられており、山口社は十四社あるがその内葛上郡の鴨山口神社が本社であるとされている。また、下鴨社（[[鴨都波神社]]）に対して上鴨社とも呼ばれていた。 [[近代社格制度]]では村社に列格した。 === 神階 === * [[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]、従五位下より正五位下（『[[日本三代実録]]』）- 表記は「鴨山口神」。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === *木造大日霊命坐像（おおひるめおのみことざぞう）・御霊大神坐像（ごりょうおおかみざぞう）{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/0000001345.html |title=国指定文化財 |publisher=御所市 |accessdate=2019-08-25 }} == 境内 == Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Sakuragisha.jpg|桜木社（稲荷神社） Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Kasugajinja.jpg|春日神社 Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Tenmanjinja.jpg|天満神社 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 社頭案内板 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001412.html 鴨山口神社] - 御所市観光ガイド * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22710 鴨山口神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かもやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|御所市|Q847334}}櫛羅2428 |ISO = JP-23 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 27|緯度秒 = 40 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 54 |祭神 = {{jalink|大山祇命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（大）
旧{{jalink|村社}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 一間社{{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|7月16日|Q2731}}（夏季大祭）
{{jalink|10月16日|Q2963}}（秋季大祭） |神事 = |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[画像:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''鴨山口神社'''（かもやまぐちじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|御所市|Q847334}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内大社|Q134917288}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社}}。 == 祭神 == '''{{jalink|大山祇命}}'''を主祭神とし、{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|大日霊大神}}、御霊大神を配祀する。 == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建年代は不詳。{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}では、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}において「{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|葛上郡|Q11620858}} 鴨山口神社 大 月次新嘗」とあるほか、祈雨祭神八十五座の一座に挙げられており、山口社は十四社あるがその内葛上郡の鴨山口神社が本社であるとされている。また、下鴨社（{{jalink|鴨都波神社|Q11675614}}）に対して上鴨社とも呼ばれていた。 {{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}では村社に列格した。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）{{jalink|1月27日|Q2284}}、従五位下より正五位下（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）- 表記は「鴨山口神」。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === *木造大日霊命坐像（おおひるめおのみことざぞう）・御霊大神坐像（ごりょうおおかみざぞう）{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/0000001345.html |title=国指定文化財 |publisher=御所市 |accessdate=2019-08-25 }} == 境内 == Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Sakuragisha.jpg|桜木社（稲荷神社） Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Kasugajinja.jpg|春日神社 Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Tenmanjinja.jpg|天満神社 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 社頭案内板 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001412.html 鴨山口神社] - 御所市観光ガイド * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22710 鴨山口神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かもやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]]

Shrine grounds
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Gose City|Q847334}} Kushira 2428 |ISO = JP-23 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 27|Latitude seconds = 40 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 42|Longitude seconds = 54 |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (Major)
Former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = One-bay shrine, {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festivals = {{jalink|July 16|Q2731}} (Summer Grand Festival)
{{jalink|October 16|Q2963}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |Rituals = |Map = Japan Nara }} [[Image:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Kamoyamaguchi Shrine''' (かもやまぐちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Gose City|Q847334}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288}}, and under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|former shrine ranking system}} it was classified as a {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}''' is enshrined as the principal deity, with {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|Ōhirume no Ōkami}} and Goryō Ōkami enshrined as associated deities. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}, within the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Register of Deities}}, it is listed as “{{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}{{jalink|Katsujo District|Q11620858}} Kamoyamaguchi Shrine, Major, Monthly and Harvest Offerings,” and it is also counted as one of the eighty-five deities of the rain-prayer rituals. Although there are fourteen Yamaguchi shrines, the Kamoyamaguchi Shrine of Katsujo District is considered the head shrine. It was also called the Upper Kamo Shrine in contrast to the Lower Kamo Shrine ({{jalink|Kamitsuhajinja Shrine|Q11675614}}). Under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Divine rank === * In the 3rd year of {{jalink|Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten’an}} ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), on {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}, it was promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade ({{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}). The notation used is “Kamoyamaguchi-no-Kami.” === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden seated statues of Ōhirume-no-Mikoto and Goryō Ōkami{{Cite web|Japanese|url= https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/0000001345.html |title=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Gose City |accessdate=2019-08-25 }} === Shrine grounds === Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Sakuragisha.jpg|Sakuragi Shrine (Inari Shrine) Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Kasugajinja.jpg|Kasuga Shrine Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Tenmanjinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * Shrine information board === External links === * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001412.html Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22710 Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation” {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamoyamaguchinimasushinsha}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かもやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:pages]]

Shrine grounds
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Gose City|Q847334}} Kushira 2428 |ISO = JP-23 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 27|Latitude seconds = 40 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 42|Longitude seconds = 54 |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (Major)
Former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = One-bay shrine, {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festivals = {{jalink|July 16|Q2731}} (Summer Grand Festival)
{{jalink|October 16|Q2963}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |Rituals = |Map = Japan Nara }} [[Image:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Kamoyamaguchi Shrine''' (かもやまぐちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Gose City|Q847334}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288}}, and under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|former shrine ranking system}} it was classified as a {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}''' is enshrined as the principal deity, with {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|Ōhirume no Ōkami}} and Goryō Ōkami enshrined as associated deities. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}, within the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Register of Deities}}, it is listed as “{{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}{{jalink|Katsujo District|Q11620858}} Kamoyamaguchi Shrine, Major, Monthly and Harvest Offerings,” and it is also counted as one of the eighty-five deities of the rain-prayer rituals. Although there are fourteen Yamaguchi shrines, the Kamoyamaguchi Shrine of Katsujo District is considered the head shrine. It was also called the Upper Kamo Shrine in contrast to the Lower Kamo Shrine ({{jalink|Kamitsuhajinja Shrine|Q11675614}}). Under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Divine rank === * In the 3rd year of {{jalink|Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten’an}} ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), on {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}, it was promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade ({{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}). The notation used is “Kamoyamaguchi-no-Kami.” === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden seated statues of Ōhirume-no-Mikoto and Goryō Ōkami{{Cite web|Japanese|url= https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/0000001345.html |title=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Gose City |accessdate=2019-08-25 }} === Shrine grounds === Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Sakuragisha.jpg|Sakuragi Shrine (Inari Shrine) Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Kasugajinja.jpg|Kasuga Shrine Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Tenmanjinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * Shrine information board === External links === * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001412.html Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22710 Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation” {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamoyamaguchinimasushinsha}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かもやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:pages]]

The notation used is “Kamoyamaguchi-no-Kami.” === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden seated statues of Ōhirume-no-Mikoto and Goryō Ōkami{{Cite web|Japanese|url= https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/0000001345.html |title=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Gose City |accessdate=2019-08-25 }} === Shrine grounds === Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Sakuragisha.jpg|Sakuragi Shrine (Inari Shrine) Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Kasugajinja.jpg|Kasuga Shrine Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Tenmanjinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * Shrine information board === External links === * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001412.html Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22710 Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation” {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamoyamaguchinimasushinsha}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かもやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:pages]]

Shrine grounds
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Gose City|Q847334}} Kushira 2428 |ISO = JP-23 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 27|Latitude seconds = 40 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 42|Longitude seconds = 54 |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (Major)
Former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = One-bay shrine, {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festivals = {{jalink|July 16|Q2731}} (Summer Grand Festival)
{{jalink|October 16|Q2963}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |Rituals = |Map = Japan Nara }} [[Image:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Kamoyamaguchi Shrine''' (かもやまぐちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Gose City|Q847334}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288}}, and under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|former shrine ranking system}} it was classified as a {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}''' is enshrined as the principal deity, with {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|Ōhirume no Ōkami}} and Goryō Ōkami enshrined as associated deities. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}, within the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Register of Deities}}, it is listed as “{{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}{{jalink|Katsujo District|Q11620858}} Kamoyamaguchi Shrine, Major, Monthly and Harvest Offerings,” and it is also counted as one of the eighty-five deities of the rain-prayer rituals. Although there are fourteen Yamaguchi shrines, the Kamoyamaguchi Shrine of Katsujo District is considered the head shrine. It was also called the Upper Kamo Shrine in contrast to the Lower Kamo Shrine ({{jalink|Kamitsuhajinja Shrine|Q11675614}}). Under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Divine rank === * In the 3rd year of {{jalink|Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten’an}} ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), on {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}, it was promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade ({{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}). The notation used is “Kamoyamaguchi-no-Kami.” === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden seated statues of Ōhirume-no-Mikoto and Goryō Ōkami{{Cite web|Japanese|url= https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/0000001345.html |title=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Gose City |accessdate=2019-08-25 }} === Shrine grounds === Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Sakuragisha.jpg|Sakuragi Shrine (Inari Shrine) Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Kasugajinja.jpg|Kasuga Shrine Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Tenmanjinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * Shrine information board === External links === * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001412.html Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22710 Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation” {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamoyamaguchinimasushinsha}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かもやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:pages]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334}} Kushira 2428 |ISO = JP-23 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 27|Latitude seconds = 40 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 42|Longitude seconds = 54 |Enshrined deity = {{ill||lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (Major)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = One-bay shrine, {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festivals = {{ill|lt=July 16|Q2731|WD=Q2731}} (Summer Grand Festival)
{{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |Rituals = |Map = Japan Nara }} [[Image:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Kamoyamaguchi Shrine''' (かもやまぐちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill||lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}''' is enshrined as the principal deity, with {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume no Ōkami}} and Goryō Ōkami enshrined as associated deities. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, within the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, it is listed as “{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}{{ill|lt=Katsujo District|Q11620858|WD=Q11620858}} Kamoyamaguchi Shrine, Major, Monthly and Harvest Offerings,” and it is also counted as one of the eighty-five deities of the rain-prayer rituals. Although there are fourteen Yamaguchi shrines, the Kamoyamaguchi Shrine of Katsujo District is considered the head shrine. It was also called the Upper Kamo Shrine in contrast to the Lower Kamo Shrine ({{ill|lt=Kamitsuhajinja Shrine|Q11675614|WD=Q11675614}}). Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Divine rank === * In the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), on {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, it was promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}). The notation used is “Kamoyamaguchi-no-Kami.” === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden seated statues of Ōhirume-no-Mikoto and Goryō Ōkami{{Cite web|Japanese|url= https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/0000001345.html |title=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Gose City |accessdate=2019-08-25 }} === Shrine grounds === Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Sakuragisha.jpg|Sakuragi Shrine (Inari Shrine) Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Kasugajinja.jpg|Kasuga Shrine Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Tenmanjinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * Shrine information board === External links === * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001412.html Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22710 Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation” {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamoyamaguchinimasushinsha}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かもやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:pages]]

Kamoyamaguchi Shrine |Image = [[File:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Keidai.jpg|270px]]
Shrine grounds
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334}} Kushira 2428 |ISO = JP-23 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 27|Latitude seconds = 40 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 42|Longitude seconds = 54 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (Major)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = One-bay shrine, {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festivals = {{ill|lt=July 16|Q2731|WD=Q2731}} (Summer Grand Festival)
{{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |Rituals = |Map = Japan Nara }} [[Image:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Kamoyamaguchi Shrine''' (かもやまぐちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' is enshrined as the principal deity, with {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume no Ōkami}} and Goryō Ōkami enshrined as associated deities. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, within the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, it is listed as “{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}{{ill|lt=Katsujo District|Q11620858|WD=Q11620858}} Kamoyamaguchi Shrine, Major, Monthly and Harvest Offerings,” and it is also counted as one of the eighty-five deities of the rain-prayer rituals. Although there are fourteen Yamaguchi shrines, the Kamoyamaguchi Shrine of Katsujo District is considered the head shrine. It was also called the Upper Kamo Shrine in contrast to the Lower Kamo Shrine ({{ill|lt=Kamitsuhajinja Shrine|Q11675614|WD=Q11675614}}). Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Divine rank === * In the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), on {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, it was promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}). The notation used is “Kamoyamaguchi-no-Kami.” === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden seated statues of Ōhirume-no-Mikoto and Goryō Ōkami{{Cite web|Japanese|url= https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/0000001345.html |title=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Gose City |accessdate=2019-08-25 }} === Shrine grounds === Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Sakuragisha.jpg|Sakuragi Shrine (Inari Shrine) Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Kasugajinja.jpg|Kasuga Shrine Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Tenmanjinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * Shrine information board === External links === * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001412.html Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22710 Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation” {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamoyamaguchinimasushinsha}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かもやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |Rituals = |Map = Japan Nara }} [[Image:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Kamoyamaguchi Shrine''' (かもやまぐちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' is enshrined as the principal deity, with {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume no Ōkami}} and Goryō Ōkami enshrined as associated deities. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}, within the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities}}, it is listed as “{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}{{ill|lt=Katsujo District|Q11620858|WD=Q11620858}} Kamoyamaguchi Shrine, Major, Monthly and Harvest Offerings,” and it is also counted as one of the eighty-five deities of the rain-prayer rituals. Although there are fourteen Yamaguchi shrines, the Kamoyamaguchi Shrine of Katsujo District is considered the head shrine. It was also called the Upper Kamo Shrine in contrast to the Lower Kamo Shrine ({{ill|lt=Kamitsuhajinja Shrine|Q11675614|WD=Q11675614}}). Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Divine rank === * In the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), on {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, it was promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}). The notation used is “Kamoyamaguchi-no-Kami.” === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden seated statues of Ōhirume-no-Mikoto and Goryō Ōkami{{Cite web|Japanese|url= https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/0000001345.html |title=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Gose City |accessdate=2019-08-25 }} === Shrine grounds === Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Sakuragisha.jpg|Sakuragi Shrine (Inari Shrine) Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Kasugajinja.jpg|Kasuga Shrine Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Tenmanjinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * Shrine information board === External links === * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001412.html Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22710 Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation” {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamoyamaguchinimasushinsha}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かもやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:pages]]

The notation used is “Kamoyamaguchi-no-Kami.” === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden seated statues of Ōhirume-no-Mikoto and Goryō Ōkami{{Cite web|Japanese|url= https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/0000001345.html |title=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Gose City |accessdate=2019-08-25 }} === Shrine grounds === Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Sakuragisha.jpg|Sakuragi Shrine (Inari Shrine) Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Kasugajinja.jpg|Kasuga Shrine Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Tenmanjinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * Shrine information board === External links === * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001412.html Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22710 Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation” {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamoyamaguchinimasushinsha}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かもやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:pages]]

Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = One-bay shrine, {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festivals = {{ill|lt=July 16|Q2731|WD=Q2731|1=July 16}} (Summer Grand Festival)
{{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963|1=October 16}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |Rituals = |Map = Japan Nara }} [[Image:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Kamoyamaguchi Shrine''' (かもやまぐちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}''' is enshrined as the principal deity, with {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume no Ōkami|1=Amaterasu}} and Goryō Ōkami enshrined as associated deities. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, within the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}{{ill|lt=Katsujo District|Q11620858|WD=Q11620858|1=Katsujō district}} Kamoyamaguchi Shrine, Major, Monthly and Harvest Offerings,” and it is also counted as one of the eighty-five deities of the rain-prayer rituals. Although there are fourteen Yamaguchi shrines, the Kamoyamaguchi Shrine of Katsujo District is considered the head shrine. It was also called the Upper Kamo Shrine in contrast to the Lower Kamo Shrine ({{ill|lt=Kamitsuhajinja Shrine|Q11675614|WD=Q11675614|1=Kamo Tsuba Shrine}}). Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Divine rank === * In the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), on {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, it was promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}). The notation used is “Kamoyamaguchi-no-Kami.” === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden seated statues of Ōhirume-no-Mikoto and Goryō Ōkami{{Cite web|Japanese|url= https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/0000001345.html |title=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Gose City |accessdate=2019-08-25 }} === Shrine grounds === Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Sakuragisha.jpg|Sakuragi Shrine (Inari Shrine) Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Kasugajinja.jpg|Kasuga Shrine Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Tenmanjinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * Shrine information board === External links === * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001412.html Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22710 Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation” {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かもやまくちにますしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:pages]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}} Kushira 2428 |ISO = JP-23 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 27|Latitude seconds = 40 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 42|Longitude seconds = 54 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Major)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = One-bay shrine, {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festivals = {{ill|lt=July 16|Q2731|WD=Q2731|1=July 16}} (Summer Grand Festival)
{{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963|1=October 16}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |Rituals = |Map = Japan Nara }} [[Image:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Kamoyamaguchi Shrine''' (かもやまぐちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}''' is enshrined as the principal deity, with {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume no Ōkami|1=Amaterasu}} and Goryō Ōkami enshrined as associated deities. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, within the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}{{ill|lt=Katsujo District|Q11620858|WD=Q11620858|1=Katsujō district}} Kamoyamaguchi Shrine, Major, Monthly and Harvest Offerings,” and it is also counted as one of the eighty-five deities of the rain-prayer rituals. Although there are fourteen Yamaguchi shrines, the Kamoyamaguchi Shrine of Katsujo District is considered the head shrine. It was also called the Upper Kamo Shrine in contrast to the Lower Kamo Shrine ({{ill|lt=Kamitsuhajinja Shrine|Q11675614|WD=Q11675614|1=Kamo Tsuba Shrine}}). Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Divine rank === * In the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), on {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, it was promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}). The notation used is “Kamoyamaguchi-no-Kami.” === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden seated statues of Ōhirume-no-Mikoto and Goryō Ōkami{{Cite web|Japanese|url= https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/0000001345.html |title=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Gose City |accessdate=2019-08-25 }} === Shrine grounds === Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Sakuragisha.jpg|Sakuragi Shrine (Inari Shrine) Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Kasugajinja.jpg|Kasuga Shrine Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Tenmanjinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * Shrine information board === External links === * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001412.html Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22710 Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation” {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]] [[Category:pages]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}} Kushira 2428 |ISO = JP-23 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 27|Latitude seconds = 40 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 42|Longitude seconds = 54 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Major)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = One-bay shrine, {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festivals = {{ill|lt=July 16|Q2731|WD=Q2731|1=July 16}} (Summer Grand Festival)
{{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963|1=October 16}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |Rituals = |Map = Japan Nara }} [[Image:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Kamoyamaguchi Shrine''' (かもやまぐちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}''' is enshrined as the principal deity, with {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume no Ōkami|1=Amaterasu}} and Goryō Ōkami enshrined as associated deities. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, within the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}{{ill|lt=Katsujo District|Q11620858|WD=Q11620858|1=Katsujō district}} Kamoyamaguchi Shrine, Major, Monthly and Harvest Offerings,” and it is also counted as one of the eighty-five deities of the rain-prayer rituals. Although there are fourteen Yamaguchi shrines, the Kamoyamaguchi Shrine of Katsujo District is considered the head shrine. It was also called the Upper Kamo Shrine in contrast to the Lower Kamo Shrine ({{ill|lt=Kamitsuhajinja Shrine|Q11675614|WD=Q11675614|1=Kamo Tsuba Shrine}}). Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Divine rank === * In the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), on {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, it was promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}). The notation used is “Kamoyamaguchi-no-Kami.” === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden seated statues of Ōhirume-no-Mikoto and Goryō Ōkami{{Cite web|Japanese|url= https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/0000001345.html |title=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Gose City |accessdate=2019-08-25 }} === Shrine grounds === Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Sakuragisha.jpg|Sakuragi Shrine (Inari Shrine) Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Kasugajinja.jpg|Kasuga Shrine Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Tenmanjinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * Shrine information board === External links === * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001412.html Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22710 Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation” {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:御所市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963|1=October 16}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |Rituals = |Map = Japan Nara }} [[Image:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Kamoyamaguchi Shrine''' (かもやまぐちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}''' is enshrined as the principal deity, with {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume no Ōkami|1=Amaterasu}} and Goryō Ōkami enshrined as associated deities. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, within the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}{{ill|lt=Katsujo District|Q11620858|WD=Q11620858|1=Katsujō district}} Kamoyamaguchi Shrine, Major, Monthly and Harvest Offerings,” and it is also counted as one of the eighty-five deities of the rain-prayer rituals. Although there are fourteen Yamaguchi shrines, the Kamoyamaguchi Shrine of Katsujo District is considered the head shrine. It was also called the Upper Kamo Shrine in contrast to the Lower Kamo Shrine ({{ill|lt=Kamitsuhajinja Shrine|Q11675614|WD=Q11675614|1=Kamo Tsuba Shrine}}). Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Divine rank === * In the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), on {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, it was promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}). The notation used is “Kamoyamaguchi-no-Kami.” === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden seated statues of Ōhirume-no-Mikoto and Goryō Ōkami{{Cite web|Japanese|url= https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/0000001345.html |title=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Gose City |accessdate=2019-08-25 }} === Shrine grounds === Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Sakuragisha.jpg|Sakuragi Shrine (Inari Shrine) Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Kasugajinja.jpg|Kasuga Shrine Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Tenmanjinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * Shrine information board === External links === * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001412.html Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22710 Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation” {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]]

The notation used is “Kamoyamaguchi-no-Kami.” === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden seated statues of Ōhirume-no-Mikoto and Goryō Ōkami{{Cite web|Japanese|url= https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/0000001345.html |title=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Gose City |accessdate=2019-08-25 }} === Shrine grounds === Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Sakuragisha.jpg|Sakuragi Shrine (Inari Shrine) Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Kasugajinja.jpg|Kasuga Shrine Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Tenmanjinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * Shrine information board === External links === * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001412.html Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22710 Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation” {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]]

Shrine grounds
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|qid=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}} Kushira 2428 |ISO = JP-23 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 27|Latitude seconds = 40 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 42|Longitude seconds = 54 |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (Major)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = One-bay shrine, {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festivals = {{ill|lt=July 16|Q2731|qid=Q2731|1=July 16}} (Summer Grand Festival)
{{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|qid=Q2963|1=October 16}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |Rituals = |Map = Japan Nara }} [[Image:Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Kamoyamaguchi Shrine''' (かもやまぐちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|qid=Q847334|1=Gose, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288|qid=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}''' is enshrined as the principal deity, with {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume no Ōkami|1=Amaterasu}} and Goryō Ōkami enshrined as associated deities. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, within the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “{{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}{{ill|lt=Katsujo District|Q11620858|qid=Q11620858|1=Katsujō district}} Kamoyamaguchi Shrine, Major, Monthly and Harvest Offerings,” and it is also counted as one of the eighty-five deities of the rain-prayer rituals. Although there are fourteen Yamaguchi shrines, the Kamoyamaguchi Shrine of Katsujo District is considered the head shrine. It was also called the Upper Kamo Shrine in contrast to the Lower Kamo Shrine ({{ill|lt=Kamitsuhajinja Shrine|Q11675614|qid=Q11675614|1=Kamo Tsuba Shrine}}). Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Divine rank === * In the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|qid=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}), on {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|qid=Q2284|1=January 27}}, it was promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}). The notation used is “Kamoyamaguchi-no-Kami.” === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Wooden seated statues of Ōhirume-no-Mikoto and Goryō Ōkami{{Cite web|Japanese|url= https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/0000001345.html |title=Nationally Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Gose City |accessdate=2019-08-25 }} === Shrine grounds === Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Sakuragisha.jpg|Sakuragi Shrine (Inari Shrine) Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Kasugajinja.jpg|Kasuga Shrine Kamoyamaguchi Jinja Tenmanjinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * Shrine information board === External links === * [http://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001412.html Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Gose City Tourism Guide * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22710 Kamoyamaguchi Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation” {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]]
